sentence_reviews.text: Stochastic gradient descent estimates gradients from minibatches; momentum and related variants accumulate update direction to reduce noise and accelerate consistent descent.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Chapter 8.1-8.3 on stochastic gradient descent, minibatches, and momentum.
sentence_reviews.notes: Minibatch gradient estimates and momentum accumulation are directly covered. The noise-reduction/consistent-descent wording is an explanatory synthesis.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of SGD, momentum & stochastic optimization is to connect Optimization theory, Calculus & automatic differentiation theory to downstream capabilities including Adam, Adafactor & adaptive optimizers.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Chapter 8 optimization, SGD, momentum, and adaptive learning-rate algorithms.
sentence_reviews.notes: The source supports SGD/momentum as optimization methods using gradients and places adaptive algorithms in the same optimization family. The graph-bridge wording is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Adam, Adafactor & adaptive optimizers
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Chapter 8.5 adaptive learning rates, including AdaGrad, RMSProp, and Adam.
sentence_reviews.notes: Adaptive gradient methods are supported as optimization variants, but Adafactor is not covered by the 2016 source and the 'substrate' framing is broader than the evidence.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Chapter 8 'Optimization for Training Deep Models'.
sentence_reviews.notes: SGD and momentum are presented as optimization algorithms; the wording is atlas synthesis.

sentence_reviews.text: Results are sensitive to data quality, hyperparameters, compute budget, random variation, and evaluation contamination. Its direct dependencies include Optimization theory, Calculus & automatic differentiation theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Chapters 5, 8, and 11 on generalization, hyperparameters, optimization, and methodology.
sentence_reviews.notes: Optimization and hyperparameter sensitivity are supported. Data quality, compute budget, random variation, and evaluation contamination are generic limitations not directly supported by this packet source.

sentence_reviews.text: SGD, momentum & stochastic optimization uses Optimization theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Chapter 8 optimization for training deep models.
sentence_reviews.notes: SGD and momentum are optimization algorithms; exact wording is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.text: SGD, momentum & stochastic optimization uses Calculus & automatic differentiation theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Chapters 6.5 and 8 on gradients/backpropagation and gradient-based optimization.
sentence_reviews.notes: Gradient-based updates depend on derivatives; automatic differentiation is part of the training context. The exact phrasing is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.text: SGD, momentum & stochastic optimization uses Datasets, sampling & splits as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Chapter 8 on minibatch stochastic gradient methods; PML data and validation/cross-validation topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Minibatch stochastic estimates require sampled training examples. Dataset splits are not essential to the update rule, so the rationale is broader than direct evidence.

edge_reviews.notes: SGD and momentum are core optimization methods for training models.
Gradient estimates and derivative-based updates make calculus/autodiff a core dependency.
Stochastic/minibatch optimization uses sampled training examples; common strength is defensible.
